title: Publisher Buffers, Drain, Admission, and Bounded Batching · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for publisher buffers, drain, admission, and bounded batching and requires the smallest defensible publisher admission and buffer-pressure controller with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for publisher admission and buffer-pressure controller. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat amqplib publish and sendToQueue return values as writable-buffer admission signals rather than delivery confirmations or Promises.
Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks.
Propagate publisher capacity to HTTP or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish Boolean Contract · changed case · Quiz Publish Boolean Contract
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 3507 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to propagate blocked, byte capacity, publish boolean, and drain state into upstream admission; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then change one routing segment and prove the exact destination set. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat amqplib publish and sendToQueue return values as writable-buffer admission signals rather than delivery confirmations or Promises.
